title: Trump Is Losing Ground With White Working-Class Voters on the Economy
summary: A review of polling data shows an extraordinary swing among white working-class voters on the president’s handling of the economy.

title: At the Kennedy Center, a Name Change Shrouded in Uncertainty
summary: President Trump’s name was removed from the arts institution’s facade overnight on Saturday. Many questions remain, including whether or not it stays off.

title: Trump Administration Reignites Its Feud With Anthropic Over Latest A.I. Models
summary: The administration’s surprise restrictions Friday cut off foreign access to Anthropic’s latest models and sparked another round of finger pointing.

title: Trump Again Picks Personal Lawyer for a Top Job, as U.S. Attorney in Manhattan
summary: James M. McDonald, a veteran former federal prosecutor and regulator, has more recently been part of President Trump’s legal team, appealing his criminal conviction.

title: New Yorkers Waited 53 Years for This. Time to Celebrate.
summary: With a championship run for the ages, the Knicks awakened joy in a city desperate for a winner.

title: Germany and Japan Are Rearming Again, 80 Years After World War II
summary: After becoming allies to disastrous effect in the 1940s, Berlin and Tokyo are finding new reasons to team up — including rebuilding their militaries.

title: Seth Rogen Knows the Secret to Marriage — and Being Rich in Hollywood
summary: After three decades in the industry, the actor-writer-director-producer has figured a lot out.

title: Bob Dylan and Liza Minnelli Already Turned 80. They Have Thoughts for Trump.
summary: “I feel the same as I did 50 years ago,” President Trump said. “It’s crazy.”

title: Detroit Is the Last City That Knows How to Dance
summary: Dance floors are flooded with selfies and poseurs. But in one city, it’s still a place to feel free.

title: Japan Is Running Out of Royals. Are More Men the Answer?
summary: Japan’s legislature is drafting a plan to allow the imperial family to adopt distant male relatives. But some in Japan would prefer a female emperor.
